{"id":"project-LICHEN-34el","title":"EDHOC: hkdf_expand counter overflow for very large okm_len","description":"CORRECTNESS: hkdf_expand counter variable is uint8_t but could wrap around for very large outputs.

Location: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:139

uint8_t counter = 1;

The loop at lines 142-171 increments counter each iteration. For okm_len approaching 255*32=8160 bytes:
- counter starts at 1
- Iterates ~255 times
- counter reaches 255, then wraps to 0

The check at line 133 (okm_len > 255 * 32) prevents this, so this is actually safe.

However, the current implementation has counter wrap-around potential if the bounds check is ever weakened.

Impact: None currently due to bounds check, but defensive improvement suggested.

Fix: Use uint16_t for counter, or add comment explaining counter won't overflow due to bounds check.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:139","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:46:22.937374Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:46:22.937403Z"}
